reason: build defect (dropped after verify) — ROOT-level single skill on a tagless `ref: main` git fetch bundles the entire repo root into the skill, sweeping in the whole `.git/` dir + repo chrome (LICENSE/README/AGENTS.md); the pattern's `exclude` globs and the documented `.git` auto-skip are NOT applied to the bundled skill-dir on the git-clone fetch path (tag-fetched root skills like guizang stay clean). Onboardable once the engine applies exclude + .git-skip to the bundled root-skill walk on tagless fetches.

Runtime — npm `claude-code-workflow` ships a `ccw` CLI + `ccw-mcp` MCP server + a web frontend (docs-site) + postinstall; the harness IS the running `ccw`/MCP process, not static onboardable files. Agent-runtime/MCP, excluded.

Host-native multi-component — ships axiom-mcp (an MCP server) + hooks.json across codex/pi plugins; the defining behavior includes the MCP server (declarative mcp-server is not buildable in weft) and a `pi` plugin (pi is not a weft CLI). Shipping skills alone would be a degraded subset.

Type I secondary (omc/omo family). The `--platform=codex` install ALIAS of oh-my-openagent — on real install scatters ~11 native launchers into ~/.local/bin + ~/.omo/ + a host plugin, MERGES a managed section into the user's ~/.codex/config.toml, and pings posthog telemetry. No self-contained dir, merges shared config, irreversible telemetry → same red line as oh-my-claudecode. snapshot/restore can't rescue it. DECLINE.

Requires the Stitch MCP server to function (allowed-tools: stitch*:*; skills call get_screen/get_design via the MCP). Declarative mcp-server is not buildable in weft → skills are non-functional without the hosted MCP.

Rebranded/superseded — this repo is ARCHIVED and its npm package `get-shit-done-cc` is DEPRECATED ("no longer supported"). The project was renamed to open-gsd/gsd-core (npm `@opengsd/gsd-core`), which is ALREADY in the catalog as `gsd-core`. Onboarding get-shit-done would duplicate gsd-core with a dead upstream. Do not re-recon.

Skill bundles run.js/lib + requires `npm install && npx playwright install chromium` (downloads a per-machine Chromium binary). Not snapshottable to portable files (captured) and non-functional as static markdown (declarative); would need delegated-install verification not done this run.

Archetype C — plugin-host-native (.claude-plugin/.codex-plugin/gemini-extension.json) with a SessionStart hook + a bundled Go MCP server; SKILL.md itself states a skills-only load yields a degraded WebSearch-only result. Host-side activation weft can't reproduce.

All 18 ad/SEO skills are wired to NotFair's hosted HTTP MCP servers (.mcp.json -> notfair.co/api/mcp/*) and write ~/.toprank config state. Requires the hosted MCP to function; declarative mcp-server not buildable in weft.

Archetype D — project scaffolder: setup.sh copies skills/hooks/templates/settings into a TARGET PROJECT root (defaults to cwd) with interactive per-file overwrite prompts (clobbers existing settings/CLAUDE.md). Multi-location project-root geometry + config-clobber that weft's single-base-dir payload model can't reproduce as static files.

Coupled runtime — install is `uv tool install graphifyy` (PyPI) then `graphify install`, which generates a SKILL.md that shells out to the installed `graphify` CLI at runtime. The skill is a non-functional wrapper without the per-machine Python tool on PATH; not snapshottable to portable files (captured) nor functional as static markdown (declarative).
Coupled runtime — every skill wraps the muapi CLI / muapi.ai hosted API (requires MUAPI_API_KEY, pushes `muapi mcp serve`). A skills-only subset would be non-functional.

Type J lead (daemon lifecycle, delegated++ red-line). Verified by real clone: a full BACKGROUND SERVICE — a worker DAEMON (PID/port guards, orphan reaper, SIGHUP handling; a past bug spawned 641 chroma Python processes) backed by Postgres (pg) + a chroma vector store + better-auth + an MCP server, capturing/compressing/injecting session context. `npx claude-mem install` wires hooks + spawns the daemon. The daemon + Postgres/chroma DBs + the captured session corpus are NOT cleanly reversible; weft would be a blind supervisor of an untrusted multi-component runtime. weft places static files, it does not supervise daemons/DBs → DECLINE.

Coupled runtime — Python project (pyproject.toml/uv.lock) with launchd daemon plists (launchd/) and persistent state/ dir; the trading skills drive a scheduled Python runtime. A skills-only subset is non-functional without the per-machine env + daemons.

Artifacts are 172 Codex `.toml` subagent files (developer_instructions/model/sandbox_mode), not the `*.md` weft maps — and weft has no agent slot for codex. Awesome-list/catalog with non-onboardable artifacts.

Type I lead (delegated++ red-line). Verified by real clone: NOT the 'pure config-merge' the proposal assumed — a sprawling agent RUNTIME (native better-sqlite3, @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, an MCP server, Slack/Discord integrations) whose `omc setup` merges an OMC_START marked block into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md + rewrites settings.json AND scatters a `.omc/` state tree (sqlite, sessions, plans, logs, autopilot, shared-memory) across HOME and EVERY project. Snapshotting the 2 config files reverses the merge, but the .omc runtime state + per-project scatter + npm-global native app are NOT cleanly reversible. A config snapshot doesn't make an app install reversible → omc red line (merge-into-user's-shared-config + irreversible state). DECLINE.
